steps.title: Find the key signal in the finding
steps.content:
The researchers found that bulkheads are more strongly negatively correlated with waterbird community integrity than riprap.

In plain terms: bulkheads damage bird communities more than riprap does.

To illustrate this, you need a direct comparison showing that when bulkheads dominate, IWCI scores drop sharply, but when riprap dominates, scores stay higher.

steps.title: Match the comparison to the finding
steps.content:
The correct answer compares Old Road (a high-bulkhead site with lower IWCI scores) to Miles (a high-riprap site with higher IWCI scores).

This directly shows bulkheads correlating more negatively with bird integrity than riprap.

Comparing two bulkhead-heavy sites sidesteps the bulkhead-vs.-riprap contrast. Mixing both structure types at each site muddies which structure causes the damage. Leaving the percentages at Miles unspecified fails to set up a clean comparison.

B is correct.
